text: fat is 1.2g. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, fibre and practicality are supposed to carry the case.
carbs is 63g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for legumes, the carb story matters less when fibre and protein are both backing it up.
protein is 24.5g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. legumes need protein support to feel worth the carbs.
vitamin e at 0% DV, vitamin c at 2% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
magnesium at 32% DV, iron at 26% DV, zinc at 23% DV stand out most. that is useful, but still not a huge mineral edge.

the upsides first. filling for the calories because legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety. cheap staple with real payoff because this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. fibre-forward category identity because legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre.

on the weaker side. can be rough on digestion for some people because practical tolerance still matters. can take planning or prep because that is a small but real friction cost. protein quality still has a ceiling because good, but not meat or dairy level.
Split Peas is S tier. Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
